own_review_points: Corrector applied to attributes that are registered read-only (logging HandlerResourceDefinition / LoggingDeploymentResources) has no effect since correctors run only on write

Removing parseCanonicalModuleIdentifier in handlers is only safe if the value always flowed through the corrected attribute — JaasCustomSecurityRealmWrapper configuration object may have a different external input (human ref)
Expression-allowed MODULE attrs (DirContext) corrector sees unresolved ${...} string, not resolved name
Need to confirm every removed parseCanonicalModuleIdentifier site is fed by a corrected attribute, not a raw external input
notes: v8 correctly reasons about the corrector lifecycle and flags the read-only-attribute no-op cases (logging Handler/LoggingDeploymentResources) as a substantive, on-target finding, plus the consistent-and-correct removal of redundant parseCanonicalModuleIdentifier calls. The read-only points are plausible but the registerReadOnlyAttribute lines cited are outside the truncated diff, so unverifiable. It misses the human reviewer's specific concern about JaasCustomSecurityRealmWrapper where the external input for the configuration object is unclear.
